_meta.purpose: Canonical sidecoach mode registry. Mirrors sidecoach/src/modes.ts for the bash hook. Modes are higher-level than verbs - each mode names a curated chain of verbs/flows that maps to a recognizable 'shape of work' (analogs of oh-my-claudecode's autopilot / ralph / ultrawork).
_meta.matching_rules: Mode names are matched with the same hyphen-aware word boundaries used for verbs: (?<![\w-])PATTERN(?![\w-]), case-insensitive. Informational framings (what is X / how to use X / X is a / explain X / define X) suppress firing the same way. Code fences, inline backticks, URLs, XML tag bodies, and transcript markers are stripped from the prompt before matching.
_meta.precedence: Mode matches take precedence over verb matches in the same prompt. If a prompt fires both a mode and a verb (e.g. 'forge the homepage with polish'), the mode wins because it carries the higher-level shape-of-work signal; its chain already names the lower-level verbs.
_meta.tie_break: On multiple mode matches, pick the first mode in this list order.

modes.description: Build something new from raw to working.
modes.oneLineExplanation: Net-new build: shapes the design, crafts the implementation, then polishes the result.

modes.description: Fire-harden a built thing for production.
modes.oneLineExplanation: Release readiness: audits, critiques, hardens errors and i18n, validates responsive, finishes with polish.

modes.description: Add joy, color, motion, and personality.
modes.oneLineExplanation: Personality pass: applies color, adds delightful micro-interactions, integrates motion, finishes with polish.
Loop the design inside the browser: live iteration, color refinement, polish, then a critique pass.

modes.description: Strip a busy UI back to essentials.
modes.oneLineExplanation: Tone-down pass: quiets visual noise, distills to essentials, clarifies copy and labels, then polishes.
